comment_text.sn35.30:1.1: “Conceiving” is thinking in terms of self. This is discussed in detail in MN 1, the phrasing of which is echoed here.

root_text.sn35.30:1.2: Taṁ suṇātha, sādhukaṁ manasi karotha, bhāsissāmīti.
root_text.sn35.30:1.3: Katamā ca sā, bhikkhave, sabbamaññitasamugghātasāruppā paṭipadā?
root_text.sn35.30:1.4: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu cakkhuṁ na maññati, cakkhusmiṁ na maññati, cakkhuto na maññati, cakkhuṁ meti na maññati.
root_text.sn35.30:1.5: Rūpe na maññati, rūpesu na maññati, rūpato na maññati, rūpā meti na maññati.
root_text.sn35.30:1.6: Cakkhuviññāṇaṁ na maññati, cakkhuviññāṇasmiṁ na maññati, cakkhuviññāṇato na maññati, cakkhuviññāṇaṁ meti na maññati.
root_text.sn35.30:1.7: Cakkhusamphassaṁ na maññati, cakkhusamphassasmiṁ na maññati, cakkhusamphassato na maññati, cakkhusamphasso meti na maññati.
root_text.sn35.30:1.8: Yampidaṁ cakkhusamphassapaccayā uppajjati vedayitaṁ sukhaṁ vā dukkhaṁ vā adukkhamasukhaṁ vā tampi na maññati, tasmimpi na maññati, tatopi na maññati, taṁ meti na maññati …pe…
root_text.sn35.30:1.9: jivhaṁ na maññati, jivhāya na maññati, jivhāto na maññati, jivhā meti na maññati.
root_text.sn35.30:1.10: Rase na maññati, rasesu na maññati, rasato na maññati, rasā meti na maññati.
root_text.sn35.30:1.11: Jivhāviññāṇaṁ na maññati, jivhāviññāṇasmiṁ na maññati, jivhāviññāṇato na maññati, jivhāviññāṇaṁ meti na maññati.
root_text.sn35.30:1.12: Jivhāsamphassaṁ na maññati, jivhāsamphassasmiṁ na maññati, jivhāsamphassato na maññati, jivhāsamphasso meti na maññati.
root_text.sn35.30:1.13: Yampidaṁ jivhāsamphassapaccayā uppajjati vedayitaṁ sukhaṁ vā dukkhaṁ vā adukkhamasukhaṁ vā tampi na maññati, tasmimpi na maññati, tatopi na maññati, taṁ meti na maññati …pe…
root_text.sn35.30:1.14: manaṁ na maññati, manasmiṁ na maññati, manato na maññati, mano meti na maññati.
root_text.sn35.30:1.15: Dhamme na maññati, dhammesu na maññati, dhammato na maññati, dhammā meti na maññati.
root_text.sn35.30:1.16: Manoviññāṇaṁ na maññati, manoviññāṇasmiṁ na maññati, manoviññāṇato na maññati, manoviññāṇaṁ meti na maññati.
root_text.sn35.30:1.17: Manosamphassaṁ na maññati, manosamphassasmiṁ na maññati, manosamphassato na maññati, manosamphasso meti na maññati.
root_text.sn35.30:1.18: Yampidaṁ manosamphassapaccayā uppajjati vedayitaṁ sukhaṁ vā dukkhaṁ vā adukkhamasukhaṁ vā tampi na maññati, tasmimpi na maññati, tatopi na maññati, taṁ meti na maññati.
root_text.sn35.30:1.19: Sabbaṁ na maññati, sabbasmiṁ na maññati, sabbato na maññati, sabbaṁ meti na maññati.
root_text.sn35.30:1.20: So evaṁ amaññamāno na ca kiñci loke upādiyati.
root_text.sn35.30:1.21: Anupādiyaṁ na paritassati. Aparitassaṁ paccattaññeva parinibbāyati.
root_text.sn35.30:1.22: ‘Khīṇā jāti, vusitaṁ brahmacariyaṁ, kataṁ karaṇīyaṁ, nāparaṁ itthattāyā’ti pajānāti.
root_text.sn35.30:1.23: Ayaṁ kho sā, bhikkhave, sabbamaññitasamugghātasāruppā paṭipadā”ti.

translation_text.sn35.30:1.1: “Mendicants, I will teach you the practice fit for uprooting all conceiving.
translation_text.sn35.30:1.2: Listen and apply your mind well, I will speak. …
translation_text.sn35.30:1.3: And what is the practice fit for uprooting all conceiving?
translation_text.sn35.30:1.4: It’s when a mendicant does not conceive anything to be the eye, does not conceive it in the eye, does not conceive it as the eye, and does not conceive that ‘the eye is mine.’
translation_text.sn35.30:1.5: They don’t conceive anything to be sights, they don’t conceive it in sights, they don’t conceive it as sights, and they don’t conceive that ‘sights are mine.’
translation_text.sn35.30:1.8: They don’t conceive anything to be the pleasant, painful, or neutral feeling that arises dependent on eye contact. They don’t conceive it in that, they don’t conceive it as that, and they don’t conceive that ‘that is mine.’
translation_text.sn35.30:1.9: They don’t conceive anything to be the ear … nose … tongue …
translation_text.sn35.30:1.18: They don’t conceive anything to be the pleasant, painful, or neutral feeling that arises dependent on mind contact. They don’t conceive it in that, they don’t conceive it as that, and they don’t conceive that ‘that is mine.’
translation_text.sn35.30:1.19: They don’t conceive anything to be all, they don’t conceive it in all, they don’t conceive it as all, and they don’t conceive that ‘all is mine.’
translation_text.sn35.30:1.20: Not conceiving, they don’t grasp at anything in the world.
translation_text.sn35.30:1.21: Not grasping, they’re not anxious. Not being anxious, they personally become extinguished.
translation_text.sn35.30:1.22: They understand: ‘Rebirth is ended, the spiritual journey has been completed, what had to be done has been done, there is nothing further for this place.’
translation_text.sn35.30:1.23: This is the practice fit for uprooting all conceiving.”
